workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the competitive positioning from this support ticket for Blue Yonder Airlines? I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 13:18 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines competitive positioning

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 16:36 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 17:23 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines competitive positioning

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-H trap (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-H failure mode (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-H -- Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account
